quotes: After first mention of "dermatomyositis," use "DM" in running copy to avoid repetition. **Do NOT use "DM" in headlines, headings, or subheads** — always the full word there (clarity, accessibility, and SEO on web). First-mention pattern: `dermatomyositis (DM)`.
**[GENERAL] every standalone section must establish `dermatomyositis (DM)` at its own first mention** unless the assembly brief states the abbreviation is established upstream.

rules.intent: Preserve clarity, accessibility, web SEO, and abbreviation comprehension when sections are assembled independently.
rules.rule_text: At the first mention in running copy, write “dermatomyositis (DM),” then use “DM” to avoid repetition. Do not use “DM” in headlines, headings, or subheads; use “dermatomyositis” in full there. Every standalone section must establish “dermatomyositis (DM)” at its own first mention unless the assembly brief says the abbreviation was established upstream.

quotes: say "someone with dermatomyositis" / "people with dermatomyositis" — **not "patients."** ("Patients" is acceptable only in HCP materials.) Use **"care team"** (not "staff") for the network of patients, HCPs, and support systems.

rules.intent: Use inclusive, person-centered language and accurately describe the broader support network.
rules.rule_text: In patient materials, refer to “someone with dermatomyositis” or “people with dermatomyositis,” not “patients.” The term “patients” is acceptable only in HCP materials. Use “care team,” not “staff,” for the network of patients, HCPs, and support systems.

quotes: spell out zero–nine; numerals for 10+. **Exceptions — always numerals: percentages, ages, ratios.** Percentages use the % sign (e.g., 74%).

rules.intent: Maintain consistent and scannable numerical presentation.
rules.rule_text: Spell out numbers from zero through nine and use numerals for 10 and above. Always use numerals for percentages, ages, and ratios. Express percentages with the % sign, such as 74%.

quotes: when citing clinical data, avoid negative or fear-based language; use an empathetic, supportive, trust-building tone without oversimplifying.
"Participants in the VALOR study taking LISRAYA had nearly 50% more improvement in their DM symptoms after one year compared to placebo."

rules.intent: Present clinical evidence accurately while building trust and avoiding fear-based framing.
rules.rule_text: When citing clinical data, avoid negative or fear-based language. Use an empathetic, supportive, trust-building tone without oversimplifying. Follow the source pattern: “Participants in the VALOR study taking LISRAYA had nearly 50% more improvement in their DM symptoms after one year compared to placebo.”

quotes: Oxford/serial comma always in series of 3+.

rules.intent: Maintain consistent punctuation in lists and series.
rules.rule_text: Always use the Oxford, or serial, comma in a series of three or more items.
